channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Assembly election campaign intensifies in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal
item.description: The article covers the key developments from the 2026 Assembly election campaign across Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal. Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed a rally in Assam and attacked ...

item.title: Eight women workers killed in road accident on Jalna’s Samruddhi Highway, several injured
item.description: A fatal road accident occurred on the Samruddhi Mahamarg (Nagpur–Mumbai Expressway) near Jalna district in Maharashtra on Tuesday evening, claiming the lives of eight women labourers and injuring seve...

item.title: SUV crashes into tricycle, motorcycle, and two cars in Bulacan due to engine malfunction; six injured
item.description: In San Jose del Monte, Bulacan, a tragic road incident occurred on Tuesday night when a Mitsubishi Montero sport utility vehicle rammed into four other vehicles, leaving six people injured. The driver...

item.title: Prime Minister Albanese addresses fuel price surge and calls for community cooperation
item.description: In a rare national address, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese urged Australians to 'do their bit' amid rising petrol and diesel prices linked to the conflict in the Middle East. The three-minute pre-rec...

item.title: Historical analysis shows Mau Mau detention camps evolved from colonial penal practices
item.description: During the Mau Mau uprising between 1952 and 1960, the British colonial government detained around 150,000 Kenyans in so-called emergency camps without trial, accusing them of supporting the rebellion...

item.title: Pakistan and Norway sign bilateral carbon trading agreement under Paris framework
item.description: Pakistan and Norway have signed the country’s first bilateral carbon market agreement under Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement, marking Pakistan’s formal entry into international carbon trading. The d...

item.title: Police warn Nigerians against electoral violence ahead of 2027 general elections
item.description: The Assistant Inspector-General of Police in charge of Zone 2 Command, Olohundare Jimoh, don yarn Nigerians say make dem avoid ballot snatching and any form of electoral violence as the 2027 general e...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Mission Prepares for Crewed Lunar Flyby
item.description: NASA is set to launch its Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972. The mission will see four astronauts — Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Ca...

item.title: Fuel Hike Crisis Hits Karachi Ride-Hailing Drivers and Daily Commuters
item.description: The recent conflict between the United States and Israel against Iran has triggered a major surge in global oil prices, heavily affecting Pakistan. Ride-hailing captains in Karachi, like Amjad Ali Kha...

item.title: Security Crisis At University Of Jos: Students Trapped Amid Violence In Plateau Communities
item.description: Tension don height for Jos, Plateau State as students and residents dey cry out over wahala wey dey threat dem lives. Hoodlums don overrun some communities close to University of Jos, and students dey...

item.title: BMH 10th Anniversary Fundraiser to Support Children Needing Transplants
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital (BMH) in Dodoma is gearing up for a special fundraising event as part of its 10th anniversary celebrations. The initiative mainly aims to support children suffering from sickle...

item.title: Phivolcs reports renewed lava activity at Mayon Volcano under Alert Level 3
item.description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) reported several lava fountaining events at Mayon Volcano in Albay this week. The most recent activity occurred at 8:29 p.m. on Tuesda...

item.title: National Mountain Bike Talent Identification and Training Camp Commences in Mysuru
item.description: The Sports Authority of India, in collaboration with the Cycling Federation of India and the Mysuru District Cycling Association, has initiated a national MTB (Mountain Bike) talent identification and...

item.title: BI reminds travelers to use official eTravel website to avoid scams this Holy Week
item.description: The Bureau of Immigration (BI) has issued a warning to all travelers to remain vigilant against fraudulent websites that charge fees for the government’s electronic travel declaration system, or eTrav...

item.title: Court Confirms Divorce Finality in Muslim Marriages After Lawful Declaration by Husband
item.description: The recent judgement by the Court has clarified the legal standing of divorce in Muslim marriages. According to the ruling, when a husband declares divorce in a manner recognised by law, it becomes fi...

item.title: The rise of VoLTE and the transformation of Tanzania’s telecom sector
item.description: Tanzania’s telecommunications industry is now undergoing a major transformation, one that could greatly shape how people and businesses connect and operate in the digital economy. For many years, most...

item.title: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos highlights need for more friendlies ahead of World Cup
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has addressed the challenges of preparing for the 2026 World Cup, particularly regarding Mexico City's high altitude. South Africa recently played two friendlies against...

item.title: Advocacy group reports 5,000 emails sent to PM Luxon on gender pay gap issues
item.description: An advocacy group, Still Minding the Gap, claims that around 5,000 women sent emails to New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, submitting 'invoices' to highlight lost wages due to the gender pa...

item.title: UNIJOS Directs Students to Leave Hostels Over Security Concerns in Jos
item.description: The University of Jos don tell all their students make dem vacate the hostels sharp sharp as security tension don rise again for Jos, the capital of Plateau State. The order come on Wednesday after fr...

item.title: U.S. Markets Rally on Reports of Potential End to Iran Conflict
item.description: U.S. stock markets experienced a significant rally on Tuesday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbing 1,125 points (2.49%) to close at 46,341.51, the S&P 500 rising 2.91% to 6,528.52, and the ...
